title: rubus eriocarpus
intro: Rubus eriocarpus, also known as the wood's rose or dewberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family, characterized by its white or pink flowers and edible fruit. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and roots, which contain salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, and have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Rubus eriocarpus include its ability to alleviate pain, reduce fever, and soothe digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Rubus eriocarpus can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as used in topical applications for skin irritations and wounds.
